s: "to surface, appear" means something in german
e: Auftauchen means to surface or appear, as in 'Aus dem Nebel tauchte eine Gestalt auf' (A figure appeared out of the fog).

s: "to apply, employ" means something in german
e: Anwenden means to apply or employ something, as in 'Sie wenden einen einfachen Trick an' (They employ a simple trick).
Verfolgen means to pursue or follow, as in 'Sie verfolgt ihr Ziel hartnäckig' (She pursues her goal stubbornly).

s: "corridor, aisle, course" means something in german
e: Der Gang means a corridor, aisle, or course, as in 'Der ganze Gang steht voller Arbeitsloser' (The entire corridor is full of unemployed people).
Beeinflussen means to influence or affect, as in 'Die Motivation beeinflusst den Erfolg' (Motivation influences success).
Die Behandlung means treatment, as in 'Für diese Krankheit gibt es eine neue Behandlung' (There is a new treatment for this disease).
Das Feld means a field, as in 'Auf diesem Feld wird Mais angebaut' (Corn is grown on this field).
Außerhalb means outside, as in 'Wir wohnen außerhalb der Stadt' (We live outside the city).
Die Einrichtung means institution or furnishings, as in 'Die Einrichtung eures Wohnzimmers gefällt mir' (I like the furnishings of your living room).
Der Lauf means course or running, as in 'Das ist der Lauf der Welt' (That is the course of the world).
Krank means sick or ill, as in 'Wenn du krank bist, musst du im Bett bleiben' (If you are sick, you must stay in bed).
Dick means thick or fat, as in 'Sie trug einen dicken Pulli' (She wore a thick sweater).
Das Zentrum means centre, as in 'Im Zentrum unserer Überlegungen sollten die Bedürfnisse von Kindern stehen' (The needs of children should be at the centre of our considerations).

s: "to refuse, reject, turn down" means something in german
e: Ablehnen means to refuse or reject, as in 'Die Behörde lehnt seinen Antrag auf ein Visum ab' (The authority rejects his visa application).
Der Anschlag means an attack, as in 'Am Montag Abend ereignete sich ein Anschlag auf die Russische Botschaft' (An attack on the Russian Embassy occurred Monday evening).

s: "to occur, remind, collapse" means something in german
e: Einfallen means to occur or come to mind, as in 'Das Wort ist mir einfach nicht eingefallen' (I simply cannot remember the word).
Warm means warm, as in 'Mir ist warm' (I am warm).
Das Ausland means foreign countries, as in 'Er möchte ein Jahr lang im Ausland studieren' (He wants to study abroad for a year).
Zurückkommen means to return, as in 'Herr Heibig kommt am Montag von seiner Reise zurück' (Mr. Heibig is returning from his trip on Monday).
Zwingen means to force, as in 'Wir sind gezwungen, schärfere Kontrollen durchzuführen' (We are forced to conduct stricter controls).

s: "to promote, support" means something in german
e: Fördern means to promote or support, as in 'Durch Stipendien werden besonders leistungsstarke Studenten gefördert' (High-achieving students are supported through scholarships).
Existieren means to exist, as in 'Diese Person existiert nur in deiner Fantasie' (This person exists only in your imagination).

s: "to remove, leave" means something in german
e: Entfernen means to remove or move away, as in 'Der Zug entfernt sich von der Stadt' (The train is moving away from the city).
Traditionell means traditional, as in 'Er hat eine sehr traditionelle Meinung' (He has a very traditional opinion).
Wenden means to turn, as in 'Das Blatt wendet sich' (The tide is turning).
Der Faktor means factor, as in 'Das ist der entscheidende Faktor' (That is the decisive factor).
Empfinden means to feel, as in 'Sie empfinden sehr viel füreinander' (They feel a lot for each other).
Die Erkenntnis means realisation or discovery, as in 'Neue wissenschaftliche Erkenntnisse gibt es fast jeden Tag' (New scientific discoveries happen almost every day).

s: "to grasp, hold" means something in german
e: Fassen means to grasp or hold, as in 'Sie fasste seine Hand' (She took his hand).
Die Spur means a track or lane, as in 'Die Spuren verliefen sich im Sand' (The tracks faded into the sand).
Das Hotel means hotel, as in 'Sie haben ein Zimmer in einem kleinen Hotel gebucht' (They booked a room in a small hotel).

s: "to address, speak" means something in german
e: Ansprechen means to address or speak to, as in 'Plötzlich sprach sie mich an' (Suddenly she spoke to me).
Schwach means weak, as in 'Die Klasse erzielt nur schwache Leistungen' (The class achieves only weak performance).
Der Fehler means a mistake or error, as in 'Begehen Sie keinen Fehler!' (Don't make a mistake!).
Das Publikum means the public or audience, as in 'Das Publikum applaudierte sehr lange' (The audience applauded for a long time).
Der Berg means a hill or mountain, as in 'Die Hütte steht auf einem hohen Berg' (The hut stands on a high mountain).

s: "in view of" means something in german
e: Angesichts means in view of, as in 'Angesichts der schlechten Straßenverhältnisse beschlossen wir, den Zug zu nehmen' (In view of bad road conditions, we decided to take the train).
Niedrig means low or base, as in 'Vorsicht, dieses Zimmer ist sehr niedrig!' (Beware, this room is very low!).

s: "to secure, safeguard" means something in german
e: Sichern means to secure or safeguard, as in 'Sichern Sie Ihren Arbeitsplatz!' (Safeguard your job!).
Die Analyse means analysis, as in 'Die Analyse der Ergebnisse wird uns noch lange beschäftigen' (The analysis of the results will occupy us for a long time).

s: "to pay attention, observe" means something in german
e: Beachten means to pay attention or observe, as in 'In letzter Zeit beachtet Franz seinen Freund Ferdinand gar nicht mehr' (Lately, Franz is not paying attention to his friend Ferdinand at all).

Die Hochschule means a college or university, as in 'Die Hochschule für Grafik und Buchkunst in Leipzig hat eine lange Tradition' (The University of Graphics and Book Art in Leipzig has a long tradition).
Link means left, as in 'Andra schreibt mit der linken Hand' (Andra writes with her left hand).
Die Studie means a study, as in 'Eine andere wissenschaftliche Studie zeigt das genaue Gegenteil' (Another scientific study shows the exact opposite).
Bereit means ready, as in 'Ich bin bereit, wir können gehen!' (I am ready, we can go!).
Schneiden means to cut, as in 'Ich habe mir meine Haare selbst geschnitten' (I cut my own hair).
Der Vorsitzende means chair person, as in 'Frau Schulz ist die Vorsitzende der Arbeiterpartei' (Ms. Schulz is the chair of the Workers' Party).
Die Freizeit means spare time, as in 'Womit verbringst du deine Freizeit?' (How do you spend your spare time?).
Der Partner means a partner, as in 'Für den Tangokurs suche ich noch einen Partner' (I am still looking for a partner for the Tango class).
Die Freiheit means freedom, as in 'Jeder Mensch hat das Recht auf Freiheit' (Every person has the right to freedom).
